 {"id":"gt-cr9","title":"Harness Design & Documentation","description":"The harness (Gas Town installation directory) needs design cleanup, documentation, and tooling.

## Current Problems

1. **Shared harness confusion**: ~/ai is shared by PGT and GGT with overlapping structures
   - PGT uses ~/ai/mayor/ as town-level Mayor home
   - GGT Mayor works in ~/ai/mayor/rigs/gastown/
   - ~/ai/gastown/ has both .gastown/ (PGT) and mayor/ (git clone)

2. **Beads redirect**: ~/ai/.beads/redirect → mayor/rigs/gastown/.beads
   - This is specific to GGT's decentralized structure
   - Should be documented as an example

3. **architecture.md**: Verify rig-level mayor/rig/ is shown correctly

4. **No harness creation tooling**: Users must manually set up

## Proposed Work

- Document what a harness IS (installation directory)
- Create harness creation command or template repo
- Update architecture.md if needed  
- Create example harness configuration for docs
- Resolve PGT/GGT sharing issue","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:15:08.769961-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.253374-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.253374-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"epic"}

+{"id":"gt-l90dq","title":"gt crew at fails to detect existing crew session","description":"## Summary
`gt crew at` started a new Claude session instead of attaching to an existing one for the same crew worker.

## Reproduction
1. Have a crew worker (joe) running in gastown/crew/joe (PID 27526 on tty s030)
2. Run `gt crew at` from the same directory (gastown/crew/joe)
3. Expected: Attach to existing session on s030
4. Actual: Started a brand new Claude session (PID 67618 on s026)

## Evidence
```
# Old joe still running:
PID 27526 on s030: /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/crew/joe (started 10:22AM)

# New joe spawned by 'gt crew at':
PID 67618 on s026: /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/crew/joe (started 10:39AM)
```

## Impact
- Duplicate crew workers cause confusion
- User expected to attach to existing session, not spawn a second one
- The original session was still functional (just stuck in feed display)

## Likely cause
`gt crew at` probably doesn't check for running Claude processes in the crew directory before spawning a new one. It should:
1. Check for existing tmux sessions for this crew worker
2. Check for running `claude` processes with cwd matching the crew directory
3. If found, attach to existing session instead of spawning new one","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-28T10:52:28.917151-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-28T10:52:42.111835-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-l9g","title":"Beads epic templates for batch work patterns","description":"Optional: Define templates for common batch work patterns.

## Concept

A template encodes a workflow pattern that can be instantiated as beads:

```yaml
# templates/batch-basic.yaml
name: basic-batch
description: Simple batch work pattern
phases:
  - name: startup
    issues:
      - title: "Verify workers ready"
  - name: working
    # Actual work issues added separately
  - name: cleanup
    issues:
      - title: "Merge all branches"
      - title: "Clean up workers"
      - title: "Report to Mayor"
```

## Usage

```bash
gt spawn --template basic-batch --epic gt-u1j --workers 3
```

Creates beads epic with template phases + actual work from gt-u1j children.

## Decision Point

Templates are OPTIONAL. The core design (beads as state, multi-wave orchestration) works without templates. Templates are sugar for common patterns.

Consider deferring to P3 or dropping entirely if beads epics with dependencies suffice.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" involved - templates just pre-populate epic/issue structure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:24.399235-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:08.868396-08:00"}
